response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer (15+ years) with genuine systems-programming depth, but the core requirement — sustained, hands-on C++/STL development — is only marginally covered by a 7-month C/C++ stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a university thesis. Their low-latency, high-throughput work was done in DLang, and their recent career is firmly web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js). There is no evidence of MQTT, message brokers at industrial scale, IEC 62443/SDL practice, or industrial-automation domain exposure, all of which are central to this role.
strengths: Real low-latency, multi-threaded systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at millions of requests/sec, albeit in DLang
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source maintainership
Broad cross-domain background including embedded, desktop, and event-driven geo-data platforms
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (~7 months, over a decade ago) versus the role's 8-12 years C++-centric profile
No industrial cybersecurity, SDL, or IEC 62443 exposure; no MQTT/OPC UA/Modbus protocol experience
Recent trajectory is web/full-stack product work, a significant pivot away from the role's on-prem industrial C++ focus

key_factors.factor: No industrial security (IEC 62443) or IoT protocol background
